context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the security-essentials instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use this skill before writing ANY security-sensitive code.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never use `String.to_atom/1` on user input** — exhausts the atom table and crashes the BEAM VM

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never interpolate strings into `fragment()` or `SQL.query()`** — always use `?` parameters for fragments and `$1` for raw SQL

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never redirect to user-controlled URLs** — validate against a whitelist or use verified routes (`~p"..."`)

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Avoid `raw/1` in templates** — if HTML is required, sanitize first with HtmlSanitizeEx

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never log sensitive data** — passwords, tokens, secrets, API keys must never appear in Logger calls

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `Plug.Crypto.secure_compare/2` for token comparison** — never `==`, which enables timing attacks

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Run dependency audits after changes** — `mix deps.audit`, `mix hex.audit`, and `mix sobelow` catch known vulnerabilities

checklist.name: instruction-9
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Write code** — implement the feature following the rules above

checklist.name: instruction-10
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Run `mix sobelow`** — static analysis catches common security issues
